title: Where is the Love?

jokes.text: Now I can get rid of Stephanie.
jokes.setup: Larry receives a massage chair as a gift
jokes.punchline: His first thought is replacing his human masseuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry Sanders character moment - selfish, pragmatic, missing the human element. Shows his character through action.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Now I can get rid of Stephanie.

jokes.text: Larry, she just had a baby.
jokes.setup: Larry complains Stephanie is unreliable for not showing up
jokes.punchline: She was giving birth
jokes.explanation: Classic straight-man correction that highlights Larry's obliviousness to others' lives.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Larry, she just had a baby.

jokes.text: She was pregnant?
jokes.setup: Learning his masseuse had a baby
jokes.punchline: Larry had no idea she was pregnant
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's complete disconnection from his staff's personal lives.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: She was pregnant?

jokes.text: He said this chair is just like having sex.
jokes.setup: Hank mentions Ricardo Montalbán endorsing the chair
jokes.punchline: The comparison to sex sets up Larry's response
jokes.explanation: Sets up the next joke effectively, typical Hank oversharing.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: He said this chair is just like having sex.

jokes.text: So I'll sit in it a couple of times, it'll complain, and then I'll sell it.
jokes.setup: Chair is compared to having sex
jokes.punchline: Larry's cynical take on relationships - use, complaints, disposal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character joke that reveals Larry's cynical worldview about relationships through a perfect analogy.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: So I'll sit in it a couple of times, it'll complain, and then I'll sell it.

jokes.text: What is perfectly adorable, good as new, and has 7 tiny little dents in it?
jokes.setup: Classic riddle format
jokes.punchline: Snow White's hymen
jokes.explanation: Classic dirty riddle. Well-constructed but generic comedy club material that could be told by anyone.

dialogue.line: What is perfectly adorable, good as new, and has 7 tiny little dents in it?

jokes.text: Is he on the show? He just came in to tell us that joke?
jokes.setup: Tom Poston visits just to tell a joke
jokes.punchline: Larry's confusion at this pointless social interaction
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the bizarre social dynamics of show business.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Is he on the show? He just came in to tell us that joke?

jokes.text: What's not to feel?
jokes.setup: Asked how the massage chair feels
jokes.punchline: Larry's typically underwhelmed response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry Sanders deadpan - unable to enjoy simple pleasures.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What's not to feel?

jokes.text: But from today on, it would mean so much to me if each of you referred to me as Hank Lepstein.
jokes.setup: Hank making a formal announcement
jokes.punchline: He's changing his name to sound more Jewish
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Hank's performative approach to his newfound faith.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: But from today on, it would mean so much to me if each of you referred to me as Hank Lepstein.

jokes.text: This is more along the lines of a preemptive strike.
jokes.setup: Hank asks if he did something wrong
jokes.punchline: Artie's military metaphor for stopping problems before they start
jokes.explanation: Good Artie line - practical and slightly threatening, delivered with characteristic understatement.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: This is more along the lines of a preemptive strike.

jokes.text: He's a sweet guy. He's a genius guy. Not so pretty, but awfully sweet.
jokes.setup: Hank describing Marvin Hamlisch
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about his looks
jokes.explanation: Typical Hank oversharing with unnecessary personal commentary.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: He's a sweet guy. He's a genius guy. Not so pretty, but awfully sweet.

jokes.text: You know, the drinking and the gambling and the cheap sex, and I mean-- oh, what a waste of time.
jokes.setup: Hank describing his empty life
jokes.punchline: Lists activities most people would consider fun as wastes of time
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Hank's dramatic overreaction to finding religion.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: You know, the drinking and the gambling and the cheap sex, and I mean-- oh, what a waste of time.

jokes.text: Why don't you just lie down there, I'll get you a cold cloth, you poor bastard.
jokes.setup: Hank's emotional religious confession
jokes.punchline: Artie treats it like a medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie response - practical, slightly mocking, but not unsympathetic.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Why don't you just lie down there, I'll get you a cold cloth, you poor bastard.

jokes.text: They run this town. They run it a hell of a lot better than the Asians.
jokes.setup: Artie saying he's worked for Jewish people a long time
jokes.punchline: Casual racist comparison presented as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy showing Artie's casual prejudice dressed up as a compliment - very HBO-era edgy.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: They run this town. They run it a hell of a lot better than the Asians.

jokes.text: Morality's just gonna get in the way.
jokes.setup: Explaining why religion doesn't belong on TV
jokes.punchline: Admits morality conflicts with entertainment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satirical line about television industry cynicism - gets at the heart of show business morality.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Morality's just gonna get in the way.

jokes.text: And I don't want you to fuck that up.
jokes.setup: Artie's speech about workplace harmony
jokes.punchline: Blunt conclusion after diplomatic buildup
jokes.explanation: Good Artie moment - builds to diplomatic speech then drops the hammer with typical bluntness.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: And I don't want you to fuck that up.

jokes.text: Larry's a talk show host.
jokes.setup: Hank asks what religion Larry is
jokes.punchline: Being a talk show host is treated as a religion
jokes.explanation: Perfect satirical observation delivered with deadpan precision - captures the narcissism of show business.

dialogue.line: Larry's a talk show host.

jokes.text: You're not even Jewish. I am Jewish. No, you're not. I am Jewish!
jokes.setup: Phil challenges Hank's newfound Judaism
jokes.punchline: Childish back-and-forth argument
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation comedy showing workplace tension over religious identity.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: You're not even Jewish.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I am Jewish.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: No, you're not.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I am Jewish!

jokes.text: You complained to me because it made you look too Jewish.
jokes.setup: Phil challenging Hank's Jewish credentials
jokes.punchline: References past incident where Hank avoided looking Jewish
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reveals Hank's hypocrisy about his Jewish identity.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: You complained to me because it made you look too Jewish.

jokes.text: At least I'm smart enough not to worship a god that sits in the middle of a Chinese restaurant with a sign that says, 'Rub my belly for luck.'
jokes.setup: Hank defending his intelligence
jokes.punchline: Racist attack on Phil's Buddhism
jokes.explanation: Shows Hank's ignorance and prejudice when challenged - character comedy through offensive behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: At least I'm smart enough not to worship a god that sits in the middle of a Chinese restaurant with a sign that says, 'Rub my belly for luck.'

jokes.text: A rabbi fits more nicely into a show business environment.
jokes.setup: Beverly complaining about religious favoritism
jokes.punchline: Hank's casual anti-Christian prejudice
jokes.explanation: Shows Hank's new prejudices developing from his religious conversion - good character work.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: A rabbi fits more nicely into a show business environment.

jokes.text: Well, Phil, I can't believe you're a comedy writer, but we have to take them both on faith.
jokes.setup: Phil expressing disbelief about Hank's conversion
jokes.punchline: Artie's dig at Phil's writing ability while making a point about faith
jokes.explanation: Excellent Artie line - insults Phil while making a philosophical point, perfectly economical.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Well, Phil, I can't believe you're a comedy writer, but we have to take them both on faith.

jokes.text: This would be a real good place to hide the matzo on Passover.
jokes.setup: Showing rabbi around his office
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong understanding of Passover traditions
jokes.explanation: Shows Hank's superficial understanding of Judaism - confuses matzah with afikomen hiding.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: This would be a real good place to hide the matzo on Passover.

jokes.text: Catholic priests can't do that, which is why I believe so many of them have prostate problems.
jokes.setup: Discussing rabbis being allowed to marry
jokes.punchline: Links celibacy to prostate problems
jokes.explanation: Typical Hank pseudo-intellectual observation that's medically questionable but sounds authoritative.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Catholic priests can't do that, which is why I believe so many of them have prostate problems.

jokes.text: Yom Kippur and the 4th of July.
jokes.setup: Rabbi asks about observing high holidays
jokes.punchline: Hank includes July 4th as a Jewish holiday
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank moment - shows his complete ignorance of Jewish traditions while trying to sound knowledgeable.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Yom Kippur and the 4th of July.

jokes.text: As a Jew you're almost a Methodist.
jokes.setup: Hank admitting he's not a good Jew
jokes.punchline: Rabbi's assessment of his religious practice
jokes.explanation: Sharp religious humor - effectively captures how non-observant Hank really is.

dialogue.speaker: Rabbi Susan
dialogue.line: As a Jew you're almost a Methodist.

jokes.text: Call me Susan.
jokes.setup: Hank feeling strange calling her Rabbi Klein
jokes.punchline: Reveals the rabbi is a woman
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - audience assumes male rabbi until this reveal.

dialogue.speaker: Rabbi Susan
dialogue.line: Call me Susan.

jokes.text: You have soft skin. I mean-- I mean, for a rabbi.
jokes.setup: Hank touching the rabbi's hand
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual comment disguised as religious observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank moment - can't separate spiritual and sexual attraction.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: You have soft skin. I mean-- I mean, for a rabbi.

jokes.text: Please don't have sex with Hank before the show. It makes his blood sugar drop.
jokes.setup: Artie meeting the rabbi
jokes.punchline: Assumes she's there for sex, gives practical advice
jokes.explanation: Classic Artie - pragmatic approach to sexual mishaps, treating religious leader like any other woman.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Please don't have sex with Hank before the show. It makes his blood sugar drop.

jokes.text: A friend loveth at all times, but, um, a brother is born for adversity.
jokes.setup: Hank interrupting Larry's comedy bit
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate religious quote during show
jokes.explanation: Shows Hank's inability to separate his newfound faith from his professional duties.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: A friend loveth at all times, but, um, a brother is born for adversity.

jokes.text: Sally Field wore her hat on The Flying Nun and look at that. That was a big show.
jokes.setup: Defending wearing yarmulke on show
jokes.punchline: Compares religious headwear to TV nun costume
jokes.explanation: Typical Hank logic - compares sacred religious practice to sitcom gimmick.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Sally Field wore her hat on The Flying Nun and look at that. That was a big show.

jokes.text: That was a show about a flying nun.
jokes.setup: Hank's Flying Nun comparison
jokes.punchline: Larry points out the obvious difference
jokes.explanation: Good straight man response highlighting the absurdity of Hank's comparison.

dialogue.line: That was a show about a flying nun.

jokes.text: I'd like to tour the rest of the place.
jokes.setup: Thanking viewers for inviting him into their living room
jokes.punchline: Wants to see the bedroom/bathroom
jokes.explanation: Decent talk show host joke playing with the metaphor of 'inviting someone into your living room.'

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I'd like to tour the rest of the place.

jokes.text: I am on God's team, and this is God's cap.
jokes.setup: Comparing yarmulke to Dodgers cap
jokes.punchline: Sports metaphor for religious devotion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hank's shallow understanding through sports analogy.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I am on God's team, and this is God's cap.

jokes.text: And besides, I am-- I am looking at the postseason.
jokes.setup: Continuing sports metaphor for religion
jokes.punchline: Implies God has playoffs
jokes.explanation: Excellent extension of the sports metaphor - shows Hank's complete trivialization of faith.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: And besides, I am-- I am looking at the postseason.

jokes.text: When I was 12, I got baptized, and I didn't shut up about it until a year later when I got laid.
jokes.setup: Artie relating to Hank's religious enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Sex replaced religion as his obsession
jokes.explanation: Great Artie moment - practical wisdom about temporary obsessions delivered with characteristic bluntness.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: When I was 12, I got baptized, and I didn't shut up about it until a year later when I got laid.

jokes.text: I talk to the man upstairs on my own time and not in the middle of 'Larry's Funny Photos.'
jokes.setup: Hank surprised Artie is religious
jokes.punchline: Artie explains appropriate time and place for religion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie wisdom about keeping personal and professional separate.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I talk to the man upstairs on my own time and not in the middle of 'Larry's Funny Photos.'

jokes.text: He even made sure that he left early for midnight mass.
jokes.setup: Beverly describing Hank's Christmas party behavior
jokes.punchline: Shows his overcompensation to appear Christian
jokes.explanation: Good backstory joke showing Hank's pattern of religious performance anxiety.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: He even made sure that he left early for midnight mass.

jokes.text: Somebody has been sitting in my chair. Did they also eat your porridge, too? You know, like Goldilocks and the three bears.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about his chair
jokes.punchline: Phil makes Goldilocks reference, then explains it
jokes.explanation: Weak joke - obvious reference that Phil then over-explains, showing poor comedic instincts.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Somebody has been sitting in my chair.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Did they also eat your porridge, too? You know, like Goldilocks and the three bears.

jokes.text: But I am behind the camera, where the viewing public cannot see me.
jokes.setup: Stu admitting he's Jewish
jokes.punchline: Explains why it's okay for him but not Hank
jokes.explanation: Good industry satire about hidden Jewish identity in entertainment business.

dialogue.speaker: Stu
dialogue.line: But I am behind the camera, where the viewing public cannot see me.

jokes.text: We live in a racist society. Even behind the camera, apparently.
jokes.setup: Network executives explaining audience prejudice
jokes.punchline: Hank's pointed observation about executive cowardice
jokes.explanation: Good sharp line from Hank - rare moment of actual insight. The 'even behind the camera' addition is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: We live in a racist society. Even behind the camera, apparently.

jokes.text: You're an uncle Tom.
jokes.setup: Stu's explanation about hiding his Jewish identity
jokes.punchline: Hank uses racial term inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Shows Hank's confusion about ethnic terminology - misapplies racial concept to religious identity.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: You're an uncle Tom.

jokes.text: They just get a little-- [mimics shock] Little butt hummer.
jokes.setup: Artie suggesting electrifying Larry's chair
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates the shock effect with sound and gesture
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment with Artie's practical approach to Larry's problems.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: They just get a little-- [mimics shock] Little butt hummer.

jokes.text: Uncle Miltie never wore a yarmulke on his show and neither did Jack Benny.
jokes.setup: Artie trying to convince Hank to remove yarmulke
jokes.punchline: Reference to famous Jewish comedians who didn't advertise their religion
jokes.explanation: Good industry reference showing Artie's knowledge of comedy history. Solid argument but not particularly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Uncle Miltie never wore a yarmulke on his show and neither did Jack Benny.

jokes.text: You'll get this yarmulke when you pry it off my cold dead head.
jokes.setup: Artie demanding he remove the yarmulke
jokes.punchline: Parodies NRA slogan about guns
jokes.explanation: Good parody of famous political slogan applied to religious headwear.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: You'll get this yarmulke when you pry it off my cold dead head.

jokes.text: Be careful, she's a hot box here.
jokes.setup: Artie introducing Hank to the rabbi
jokes.punchline: Sexual comment about religious leader
jokes.explanation: Classic Artie inappropriate comment treating rabbi like any other woman.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Be careful, she's a hot box here.

jokes.text: Like the-- Like the Hebrews at Masada.
jokes.setup: Hank saying he's standing tough
jokes.punchline: Compares his workplace dispute to historic Jewish martyrdom
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Hank's tendency to dramatize everything, comparing TV job to mass suicide.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Like the-- Like the Hebrews at Masada.

jokes.text: Yeah, that sounds like a woman.
jokes.setup: Rabbi Susan rejecting his romantic advance
jokes.punchline: Hank's sexist response to rejection
jokes.explanation: Shows Hank's casual sexism when things don't go his way - character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Yeah, that sounds like a woman.

jokes.text: Yeah, that sounds like a woman.
jokes.setup: Rabbi politely rejecting Hank's romantic advance
jokes.punchline: Hank's casually sexist response to rejection
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hank's defensive sexism when rejected. Perfectly inappropriate response.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Yeah, that sounds like a woman.

jokes.text: Right on, sister.
jokes.setup: Beverly giving him encouragement about faith
jokes.punchline: Hank's inappropriate cultural appropriation response
jokes.explanation: Shows Hank's cluelessness about appropriate cultural expressions.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Right on, sister.

jokes.text: What the hell are orange Jews?
jokes.setup: Reading hate mail about 'orange jews'
jokes.punchline: Hank doesn't understand it refers to orange juice
jokes.explanation: Good moment showing Hank's confusion about anti-Semitic hate mail referencing his endorsement.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: What the hell are orange Jews?

jokes.text: Maybe he's referring to the orange juice you endorse.
jokes.setup: Hank confused about 'orange jews' reference
jokes.punchline: Artie connecting it to Hank's endorsement deal
jokes.explanation: Good practical explanation showing how Hank's religious display affects his commercial interests. Solid setup-payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Maybe he's referring to the orange juice you endorse.

jokes.text: That's what the Olsen twins say.
jokes.setup: Hank mentioning Bob Saget's dick jokes
jokes.punchline: References Saget's role on Full House with the twins
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy referencing the contrast between Saget's family-friendly TV image and his adult standup material.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: That's what the Olsen twins say.

jokes.text: [Screams, whimpering] My ass feels like it's on fire!
jokes.setup: Larry sits in his electrified chair
jokes.punchline: Gets shocked as planned, screams in pain
jokes.explanation: Payoff to Artie's earlier suggestion - physical comedy as episode conclusion.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: My ass feels like it's on fire!

jokes.text: I thought it was Phil.
jokes.setup: Larry screaming from chair shock
jokes.punchline: Artie reveals he set it up to shock Phil instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - reveals Artie's real target and adds layer to the prank.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I thought it was Phil.

tmdb_overview: Larry gets upset over a review from TV critic Tom Shales, prompting him to fire off a fax in retaliation and to seek sympathy from show guest Sally Field.
